transcript.text: Hello, living in the van vlog. January 5th, the morning version of 2012. I met the mechanic in like, uh, Stanwood, Aust- um, not, I'm not in Australia, I'm in Stanwood, America. Stanwood, Austra- uh, Stanwood, Washington. Jesus.

Okay, so anyways, um, let me tell you about this dream I had last night before I forget it, because it was weird. Okay, I had a dream that, um, I had a dream while I was listening to my audio mp3. I was sleeping and listening to the audio mp3 at the same time.

Um, because I was laying in bed, like, with insomnia. I didn't get to sleep until like 3 or something, because I was stupid and I ate way too much ramen noodles and eggs, like 4 packets and 6 eggs, even though I wasn't hungry.
And then I drank of my big alcohol drink, and a whole bunch of orange juice, and I just had insomnia. Luckily I could breathe, but, because normally that gives me acid reflux.
But, um, so anyways, I'm listening to my history of Rome, and I was listening to the, uh, the Nero section.

Super cheap hotel room, but I had my own room. It was a really big room with a big bed, and it was all set up like I was living there.

I was, like, hanging out in the town, and I go into the, into the room, into my bedroom, and I was like, okay, I want to go to sleep in the dream.
I want to go to sleep, but this, like, I want to take, I want to turn off my, I want to turn off the history of Rome, right?
But before that happens, I'm listening to the history of Rome, and I'm hearing about Nero, and I'm like, oh yeah, Nero.
So, and, and it said, yeah, and Nero, um, when Nero was first emperor, his mother, Agrippina, was, um, was the, uh, the regent, because he was too young.
And so she, she was, um, managing the affairs and entertaining, um, entertaining foreign ambassadors herself.
And I, and I thought, when I heard that, I thought, oh wow, Agrippina's entertaining foreign ambassadors.
That must be weird for the foreign ambassador.
They show up in Rome, and, oh look, it's a woman who's in charge of everything.
And then they go back to, back to their home country, and they're like, oh, yeah, it was a woman who was in charge.
And then, but then Nero got killed, and then, and then now it's not a woman, and they must have been confused.
It must have been really weird.
And I was thinking about that in a way that I wouldn't think about it, like, if I was awake.

It wasn't like there was that much confusion between the countries, you know.

Stanwood.
